text.title: grafana-dashboards Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for grafana-dashboards. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use RED (Rate, Errors, Duration) for service dashboards and USE (Utilization, Saturation, Errors) for infrastructure/hardware dashboards. Do not mix them in the same row.
rule.rationale: RED answers "is the service healthy from the user's perspective?" USE answers "is the infrastructure saturating?" Mixing them creates cognitive overhead for on-call engineers who need fast answers.

rule.statement: Structure dashboards hierarchically: summary row at top (stat panels), detail rows below (time series). Use Grafana 12 Tabs to segment cluster/namespace/pod views without splitting into separate dashboards.
rule.rationale: On-call engineers need the summary answer in under 5 seconds. Separate dashboards require navigation time; tabs keep context while segregating detail.

rule.statement: Every dashboard must have at minimum three variables: datasource (type: datasource), namespace (type: query), and interval (type: interval with auto).
rule.rationale: Datasource variable enables multi-cluster reuse. Namespace variable enables team-specific scoping. Interval variable prevents fixed time windows from producing misleading rates.

rule.statement: Use Stat panels for current values needing instant color-coded status. Use Time series for trends. Use Gauge for percentage values with min/max bounds (SLO compliance, error budget). Use Table for multi-dimensional comparisons.
rule.rationale: Choosing the wrong panel type forces users to mentally transform data. A Stat panel for a time-based trend loses context; a Time series for a single current value adds unnecessary visual noise.

rule.statement: Provision all production dashboards as Kubernetes ConfigMaps with the grafana_dashboard=1 label. Never rely on UI-created dashboards in production.
rule.rationale: UI dashboards are lost on pod restart or cluster recreation. ConfigMap-provisioned dashboards are version-controlled, reviewed in PRs, and survive infra events. The label triggers automatic discovery by Grafana's sidecar.

rule.statement: Configure Grafana to load dashboards from a folder mounted via the sidecar. Set allowUiUpdates: false in production to prevent UI drift from the Git-managed source.
rule.rationale: allowUiUpdates: true allows operators to save UI changes that are then lost on the next pod restart (they are not written back to the ConfigMap). Disabling it enforces the GitOps workflow.

rule.statement: Add deployment and alert annotations to all service dashboards. Annotations correlate metric changes with the events that caused them.
rule.rationale: Without deployment markers, engineers spend minutes correlating a latency spike to the release that caused it. Annotations make the correlation instant and reduce MTTR.
